inputs.price_source: NASDAQ placeholder — marvin_cloud_refresh updates live quote
inputs.fcf_source: FY2025: cash from operations $10.031B minus capital spending $179M, divided by 427M diluted shares (10-K FY2025)
inputs.normalization_note: Lawrence uses reported free cash flow; stock-based compensation $1.942B is already a non-cash add-back in cash from operations

bear.notes: Generative-AI competition slows net new Creative Cloud seats; Digital Experience growth decelerates; multiple compresses

base.notes: Subscription RPO $22.52B supports mid-single-digit visibility; Firefly AI embedded in Creative Cloud; buybacks offset dilution

bull.notes: AI monetization lifts ARPU; Experience Cloud share gains; sustained $10B+ annual repurchases

option_scan.question: GAAP book misstates core assets?
option_scan.evidence: Asset-light software; equity securities $3.6B marked at fair value (10-K FY2025)

option_scan.question: Backlog / RPO not in FCF path?
option_scan.evidence: RPO $22.52B; 65% recognized within 12 months — modeled via subscription growth, not separate terminal (10-K FY2025)

option_scan.question: In-business loss segment?
option_scan.evidence: All three segments profitable; consolidated operating margin 37% FY2025

option_scan.question: Embedded product option in revenue?
option_scan.evidence: Generative AI integrated into Creative Cloud and Document Cloud — growth in base scenario

option_scan.question: Material separate options after scan?
option_scan.answer: No dated NAV or binary bets
option_scan.evidence: Figma deal terminated Dec 2023; no disclosed fair-value marks on in-business AI options

growth_explanation.mechanism: Subscription RPO ($22.52B) converts to revenue; Creative Cloud seat/ARPU growth plus Experience Cloud enterprise expansion; capital returns via buybacks
growth_explanation.source: 10-K FY2025 segment note; Q2 FY2026 revenue +12% YoY H1

falsifiers.observation: RPO growth falls below revenue growth for two consecutive quarters
Management cites competitive seat churn from generative-AI alternatives in 10-Q risk update
falsifiers.action: Downgrade moat to eroding; cap exit multiple at 20x

paths.label: Growth theory (pre-falsifier)
paths.weight_why: Segment blend before falsifier pass; diagnostic only when it differs from falsifier-adjusted.
weight_falsifiers: Included at full weight alongside falsifier-adjusted without divergence note

paths.label: Bear scenario (Lawrence)
paths.weight_why: Downside stress; low weight because bear is a sensitivity, not a competing explanation of base mechanics.
weight_falsifiers: Bear assumptions become base case in filings (e.g. sustained volume collapse)
paths.hard_to_vary: yes: asymmetric low weight is structural

paths.label: Bull scenario (Lawrence)
paths.weight_why: Upside stress probe; higher than bear because optionality archetype has skew, but still below primary paths.
weight_falsifiers: Bull growth sustained in filings for 4+ quarters without falsifier trigger

qualitative_adjustments.factor: No dated predictive attribute at spot
qualitative_adjustments.rationale: Scarcity is descriptive; no timed mispricing catalyst in base
qualitative_adjustments.falsifier: Cross-check names dated catalyst with timeline

deutsch_checks.reach: Weights encode evidence tier (A primary → D external), not fitted to hit a target IRR

synthesis.notes: Capstone IRR combining filings, segments, overlays, third-party, qualitative
